programme.title: The Long Goodbye (1973)
programme.desc: Raymond Chandler's private eye Philip Marlowe meets a fun couple in the 1970s Los Angeles.

programme.title: Of Mice and Men (1992)
programme.desc: Migrant worker George (Gary Sinise) protects his strong, simple-minded friend Lennie (John Malkovich) in the 1930s California.

programme.title: Cinderfella (1960)
programme.desc: Cinderfella's fairy godfather helps him escape from his wicked stepmother and stepbrothers.

programme.title: Hot Pursuit (2015)
programme.desc: An uptight and by-the-book cop tries to protect the widow of a drug boss as they race through Texas pursued by crooked cops and murderous gunmen.

programme.title: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider (2001)
programme.desc: A beautiful globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) battles otherworldly creatures for possession of a powerful artifact.

programme.title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.sub-title: Lara Croft Tomb Raider: The Cradle of Life (2003)
programme.desc: A globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) must find Pandora's box before a maniacal scientist (Ciarán Hinds) can harness its power for world domination.

programme.title: Sahara (2005)
programme.desc: A treasure hunter (Matthew McConaughey) and his sidekick (Steve Zahn) join forces with a doctor (Penélope Cruz) to search for a Confederate ship in Africa.

programme.title: Gang Related (1997)
programme.desc: Two corrupt homicide cops selling narcotics accidentally kill an undercover DEA agent and try to pin the murder on a homeless alcoholic, but the dead man's partner comes calling.

programme.title: The Lookout (2007)
programme.desc: A brain-damaged former athlete (Joseph Gordon-Levitt) finds himself pulled into a heist at the bank where he works as a janitor.

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: Born with facial differences that, up until now, have prevented him from going to a mainstream school, Auggie Pullman becomes the most unlikely of heroes when he enters the local fifth grade.

programme.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button (2008)
programme.sub-title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button (2008)
programme.desc: Benjamin Button (Brad Pitt) is born an old man in 1918 New Orleans and ages in reverse and, in so doing, becomes intimately familiar with the natures of love and death.

programme.title: The Fire Inside (2024)
programme.desc: The story of Claressa "T-Rex" Shields, a boxer from Flint, Mich., who trained to become the first woman in her country's history to win an Olympic gold medal in the sport.

programme.title: The Rage: Carrie 2 (1999)
programme.desc: After her best friend commits suicide, an unpopular girl discovers she has telekinetic powers and uses them to seek revenge on her humiliators.

programme.title: From
programme.sub-title: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter
programme.desc: Boyd and Jade find themselves at odds over whether they can trust the knowledge in Jade's head; deeply troubling news from the settlement makes its way back to town.

programme.title: Poltergeist III (1988)
programme.desc: Destructive demons follow a little girl (Heather O'Rourke) sent to live with her aunt (Nancy Allen) and uncle (Tom Skerritt) in a Chicago high-rise.

programme.title: Summer Days, Summer Nights (2018)
programme.desc: On the south shore of Long Island in the summer of 1983, a group of working-class teenagers and 20-somethings work their summer jobs, fall in and out of love, and wrestle with what the future holds when the summer ends and the real world beckons.

programme.title: The Fortune Cookie (1966)
programme.desc: A TV cameraman hurt while covering a football game is told by his brother-in-law how to get a big insurance settlement.

programme.title: Saint Maud (2019)
programme.desc: Maud, an unstable, newly devout hospice nurse, becomes obsessed with saving her dying patient's soul -- but sinister forces and her own sinful past threaten her holy calling.

programme.title: The Visitor (2022)
programme.desc: When Robert and his wife move to her childhood home, he discovers an old portrait in the attic of a man who looks just like him. He soon travels down a frightening rabbit hole as he tries to discover the true identity of his mysterious doppelgänger.

programme.title: Preparation for the Next Life (2025)
programme.desc: A Uyghur woman moves to New York where she labors in Chinatown's underground kitchens. As she falls in love with a young soldier, they discover the possibility of a better life together than the ones they believed they were destined to live alone.

programme.title: Lenny (1974)
programme.desc: 1950s comic Lenny Bruce (Dustin Hoffman) spurs controversy with his drug use, graphic language and marriage to a stripper (Valerie Perrine).

programme.title: Nickel Boys (2024)
programme.desc: Elwood Curtis' college dreams are shattered when he's sentenced to a brutal reformatory in the Jim Crow South. Clinging to his optimistic worldview, Elwood strikes up a friendship with a fellow Black teen who dispenses fundamental tips for survival.

programme.title: From
programme.sub-title: The Heart Is a Lonely Hunter
programme.desc: Boyd and Jade find themselves at odds over whether they can trust the knowledge in Jade's head; deeply troubling news from the settlement makes its way back to town.

programme.title: Finding Neverland (2004)
programme.desc: Writer J.M. Barrie (Johnny Depp) befriends a widow (Kate Winslet) and her four sons who inspire him to create the character Peter Pan.

programme.title: Starman (1984)
programme.desc: A Wisconsin widow (Karen Allen) falls in love with an alien (Jeff Bridges) who has remade himself as a double of her husband.

programme.title: Enchanted April (1991)
programme.desc: An English novelist's wife (Miranda Richardson) rents an Italian villa with a widow (Joan Plowright) and two other women.

programme.title: American Gigolo (1980)
programme.desc: A professional Beverly Hills escort falls in love with a big shot's wife and winds up framed for murder.

programme.title: Henry V (1989)
programme.desc: Shakespeare's king of England (Kenneth Branagh) attacks France and declares his love for the French king's daughter.

programme.title: For a Few Dollars More (1965)
programme.desc: A man with no name (Clint Eastwood) and a man with a mission (Lee Van Cleef) hunt a Mexican bandit (Gian Maria Volonte) for different reasons.

programme.title: Support Your Local Gunfighter (1971)
programme.desc: A con man (James Garner) and his partner (Jack Elam) profit from mistaken identity in the mining town of Purgatory.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in the West (1968)
programme.desc: An outlaw (Henry Fonda) working for a railroad magnate fights a stranger (Charles Bronson) for a New Orleans widow's (Claudia Cardinale) land.

programme.title: The Relic (1997)
programme.desc: A homicide detective (Tom Sizemore) helps a biologist (Penelope Ann Miller) hunt a giant creature that is killing people in a Chicago museum.

programme.title: Event Horizon (1997)
programme.desc: A spaceship missing for seven years finally re-emerges and sends out a distress signal. But when a team is sent out to investigate, they discover that something sinister has taken over the craft.

programme.title: Cyborg (1989)
programme.desc: A martial artist (Jean-Claude Van Damme) hunts a killer (Vincent Klyn) in a plague-infested urban dump of the future.

programme.title: Novocaine (2025)
programme.desc: When the girl of his dreams gets kidnapped, a man turns his inability to feel pain into an unexpected advantage. He fights a bunch of thugs to get her back.

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate for money to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards joins the top-rated show on television, "The Running Man," a deadly competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins.

programme.title: A Working Man (2025)
programme.desc: Levon Cade left behind a military career in the black ops to live a simple life. However, when human traffickers kidnap his boss's daughter, his search to bring her home uncovers a world of corruption far greater than he ever could have imagined.

programme.title: The Golden Child (1986)
programme.desc: A professional child-finder (Eddie Murphy) is recruited to rescue a mystical Tibetan boy from a shape-shifting villain (Charles Dance).

programme.title: Leviathan (1989)
programme.desc: Precious-metals miners (Peter Weller, Richard Crenna, Amanda Pays) become trapped on the ocean floor with an eellike genetic alteration.

programme.title: Criminal Law (1988)
programme.desc: A Boston lawyer (Gary Oldman) gets his rich client (Kevin Bacon) off for murder, then realizes he's still out there killing.

programme.title: Something Wild (1986)
programme.desc: A wild woman (Melanie Griffith) takes a yuppie (Jeff Daniels) to her high-school reunion, attended by her ex-convict husband (Ray Liotta).

programme.title: My Dead Boyfriend (2016)
programme.desc: A woman (Heather Graham) goes on a manic quest to learn the truth about her boyfriend after she finds him dead on their couch.

programme.title: Children of a Lesser God (1986)
programme.desc: Starting his new job as an instructor at a New England school for the deaf, James Leeds meets Sarah Norman, a young deaf woman who works at the school as a member of the custodial staff.

programme.title: Elizabethtown (2005)
programme.desc: In Kentucky to bury his father, a troubled man (Orlando Bloom) gets his life on track with the help of a free-spirited flight attendant (Kirsten Dunst).

programme.title: In the Land of Saints and Sinners (2023)
programme.sub-title: In the Land of Saints and Sinners (2023)
programme.desc: Hoping to leave his dark past behind him, former assassin Finbar Murphy leads a quiet life in a coastal Irish town. However, when terrorists show up, he must soon choose between exposing his secret identity and defending his friends and neighbors.

programme.title: Valkyrie (2008)
programme.desc: Fearing that Hitler will destroy his beloved Germany, Col. Claus von Stauffenberg joins a group of high-ranking men who are plotting to assassinate him.

programme.desc: A commando squad must conduct a midair assault upon a hijacked plane loaded with terrorists and a deadly nerve gas.

programme.title: Grudge Match (2013)
programme.desc: A boxing promoter (Kevin Hart) offers two rival boxers (Robert De Niro, Sylvester Stallone) the chance to come out of retirement for one final bout.

programme.title: Last Vegas (2013)
programme.desc: Aging friends (Michael Douglas, Robert De Niro, Morgan Freeman) head to Las Vegas to celebrate the end of one's long bachelorhood and relive their glory days.

programme.title: Without a Paddle (2004)
programme.desc: Three childhood friends (Seth Green, Matthew Lillard, Dax Shepard) embark on a canoe trip to find a plane hijacker's stash of money.

programme.title: Without a Paddle: Nature's Calling (2009)
programme.desc: Two friends (Oliver James, Kristopher Turner) and a crazy Briton (Rik Young) have wild misadventures while searching for a missing girl in the woods.

programme.title: Anything for Fame
programme.desc: Venturing into the virtual Wild West to profile a new breed of content creator.

programme.title: Erotic Alien Encounter (2024)
programme.desc: A humanoid alien named David gets stranded on Earth and is befriended by a beautiful nurse named Kate. With help from her best friend, Kate is determined to help David return to his home planet before government agents get to him first.

programme.title: Get Rich or Die Tryin' (2005)
programme.desc: When his world spirals out of control, a drug dealer wants to leave his violent life behind and pursue his dream of becoming a rap artist. He channels the same manic intensity to his writing as he does to dealing.

programme.title: Undisputed (2002)
programme.desc: An undefeated world-champion prizefighter, who is convicted of rape and sent to prison, must confront and fight the reigning prison boxing champion.

programme.title: The Outlaws (2024)
programme.desc: A group of outlaws awaken to their empty train heist bag. Interrogations begin as each cowboy becomes the focus in an attempt to unmask the thief among them. Tensions mount as each bandit struggles to clear their name before bullets fly.

programme.title: Small Soldiers (1998)
programme.desc: A boy (Gregory Smith) and his neighborhood crush (Kirsten Dunst) become involved in a war when a faulty microchip turns toy soldiers into a tiny but lethal army.

programme.title: Dreamland (2019)
programme.desc: A teen bounty hunter is torn between helping or capturing a seductive fugitive bank robber hiding in his small town during the Great Depression.

programme.title: Face/Off (1997)
programme.desc: An FBI agent (John Travolta) and his nemesis (Nicolas Cage) trade physical identities.

programme.title: The Godfather (1972)
programme.desc: Crime boss Vito Corleone and his sons, the ruthless yet loyal members of the Corleone family, rule their secretive New York empire with an iron fist.

programme.title: Breach (2020)
programme.desc: Fleeing a devastating plague on Earth, an interstellar ark comes under attack from a new threat -- a shape-shifting alien force intent on slaughtering what's left of humanity.

programme.title: Virtuosity (1995)
programme.desc: An ex-cop doing prison time chases a virtual-reality killer from the program onto the Los Angeles streets.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991)
programme.desc: Two cyborgs from the future battle over the life of a teen (Edward Furlong) who will someday lead humanity against the machines.

programme.title: Terminator: Dark Fate (2019)
programme.desc: Seasoned warrior Sarah Connor joins forces with a hybrid cyborg human and the T-800 model to protect a young woman from the Rev-9 -- a newly modified and seemingly indestructible liquid Terminator from the future.

programme.title: Baby Driver (2017)
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a talented getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love and freedom.

programme.title: Old Guy (2024)
programme.desc: An aging hit man must train a young prodigy when his employer moves to replace the old guard. However, when they learn they're being betrayed, the unlikely pair turns into a lethal team -- with their double-crossing bosses in their sights.

programme.title: The Time Machine (2002)
programme.desc: A scientist (Guy Pearce) travels into the future and meets a race of people who fear hideous monsters living under the ground.

programme.title: Run & Gun (2021)
programme.desc: A former criminal gets blackmailed into one last job to collect a mysterious package. After a deadly double-cross, he soon finds himself wounded and on the run from ruthless assassins who will stop at nothing to get what he has.

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers (Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan) become pawns of a mysterious woman who threatens their friends and family and uses technology to control their actions.

programme.title: The Running Man (1987)
programme.desc: A police-state scapegoat (Arnold Schwarzenegger) must play a TV host's (Richard Dawson) deadly game show for the 21st-century masses.

programme.title: Downhill Racer (1969)
programme.desc: A cocky American skis and après-skis Europe, training with his coach for the Olympics.

programme.title: The Hunt for Red October (1990)
programme.desc: Moscow, Washington and a CIA analyst (Alec Baldwin) track a renegade Soviet captain (Sean Connery) and his new submarine.

programme.title: Faster (2010)
programme.desc: A veteran cop (Billy Bob Thornton) and an assassin (Oliver Jackson-Cohen) track an ex-convict (Dwayne Johnson) who is on a mission to take revenge on his brother's killers.

programme.title: Hot Rod (2007)
programme.desc: An accident-prone daredevil (Andy Samberg) plans an outrageous stunt to raise money for lifesaving surgery for his abusive stepfather.

programme.title: Cheech & Chong's Last Movie (2024)
programme.desc: Cheech Marin and Tommy Chong discuss their lifelong friendship and popularity as a comedic duo. Features interviews, sketches and never-before-seen footage spanning their five-decade career.

programme.title: 21 Jump Street (2012)
programme.desc: Two cops must confront their teenage angst all over again when they go undercover as high-school students.

programme.title: 22 Jump Street (2014)
programme.desc: Officers Jenko and Schmidt begin to question their partnership after each infiltrates different cliques while undercover at a local college.

programme.title: 68 Kill (2017)
programme.desc: A hardworking man's inability to say no to a beautiful woman gets him in trouble when he agrees to steal $68,000.

programme.title: Good Mourning (2022)
programme.desc: A movie star must choose between pursuing his one true love or landing a life-changing role in a film.

programme.title: Maniac (2012)
programme.desc: A serial killer (Elijah Wood) removes his victims' scalps and attaches them to the vintage mannequins he restores in his late mother's shop.

programme.title: Snag (2023)
programme.desc: After learning the woman he once loved is being held captive by ruthless gangsters, a lone wolf seeks out allies and storms into a world of violence to rescue her.

programme.title: World Trade Center (2006)
programme.desc: Two Port Authority officers become trapped in the rubble of the World Trade Center after going in to help people escape from the buildings during the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001.

programme.title: The Gambler (2014)
programme.desc: A literature professor (Mark Wahlberg) with gambling debts borrows money from a mobster (Michael Kenneth Williams) and offers his life as collateral while immersing himself in an illicit, underground world.

programme.title: Flight of the Intruder (1991)
programme.desc: Two Navy pilots (Willem Dafoe, Brad Johnson) hatch a rogue mission to bomb Hanoi with a special low-altitude plane.

programme.title: Stop-Loss (2008)
programme.desc: A soldier (Ryan Phillippe) whose tour of duty has ended takes matters into his own hands when the Army tries to force him back into active duty in Iraq.

programme.title: Danger Close (2019)
programme.desc: Australian and New Zealand soldiers face overwhelming odds as they fight for their lives during the Battle of Long Tan in Vietnam.

programme.title: Southpaw (2015)
programme.desc: After losing everything to a senseless tragedy, a boxer (Jake Gyllenhaal) turns to a retired fighter (Forest Whitaker) for guidance as he struggles toward redemption.

programme.title: A Bad Moms Christmas (2017)
programme.desc: As if creating the perfect Christmas for their families isn't hard enough, under-appreciated and overburdened moms Amy, Kiki and Carla will have to do it while hosting and entertaining their own respective mothers during the holidays.

programme.title: The Fabulous Four (2024)
programme.desc: Lifelong friends travel to Key West, Fla., to be bridesmaids in a college girlfriend's surprise wedding. Sisterhoods are rekindled, the past resurfaces, and there are enough sparks, sex and romance to change all of their lives in unexpected ways.

programme.title: Certain Women (2016)
programme.desc: Three strong-willed women (Kristen Stewart, Laura Dern, Michelle Williams) strive to forge their own paths amidst the wide-open plains of the American Northwest.

programme.title: Strange Darling (2023)
programme.desc: Nothing is what it seems when a twisted one-night stand spirals into a serial killer's vicious murder spree.

programme.title: The Fan (1981)
programme.desc: Douglas, a record salesman, is an obsessive fan of actress Sally Ross. When his letters are rejected, he strikes out at her and her loved ones.

programme.title: View From the Top (2003)
programme.desc: A woman (Gwyneth Paltrow) from a small Nevada town makes friends while training to become a flight attendant.

programme.title: Sarafina! (1992)
programme.desc: A South African student (Leleti Khumalo) joins the anti-apartheid revolution in her township of Soweto.

programme.title: Failure to Launch (2006)
programme.desc: The parents of a young man (Matthew McConaughey) who still lives at home hire a beautiful woman (Sarah Jessica Parker) to entice him to finally leave the nest.

programme.title: The Wedding Banquet (2025)
programme.desc: Hoping to stay in the country, a gay man proposes a green card marriage to a female friend in exchange for paying for her IVF treatment. However, things get complicated when his grandmother surprises them with an extravagant Korean wedding banquet.

programme.title: Kate & Leopold (2001)
programme.desc: A Manhattan woman (Meg Ryan) falls in love with the 19th-century Duke of Albany (Hugh Jackman) who has stepped through a time portal.

programme.title: Molly's Game (2017)
programme.desc: Molly Bloom runs the world's most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested by the FBI. Her only ally is defense lawyer Charlie Jaffey, who learns that there is much more to Molly than the tabloids lead people to believe.

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Diary (2001)
programme.desc: An outrageous British woman (Renée Zellweger) decides to take control of her life, but instead falls for two very different men (Colin Firth, Hugh Grant).

programme.title: The English Patient (1996)
programme.desc: A Hungarian count's (Ralph Fiennes) fling with a British newlywed leads to tragedy in World War II North Africa.

programme.title: Wakefield (2016)
programme.desc: Howard Wakefield, a New York City lawyer, hides out in the attic of his home for weeks. When a former boyfriend re-enters his wife's life, he realizes that he may not be able to return to the life he has abandoned.

programme.title: The Tribes of Palos Verdes (2017)
programme.desc: When the situation at her idyllic Palos Verdes home turns volatile, young Medina attempts to surf her way to happiness.

programme.title: Unhook the Stars (1996)
programme.desc: A suburban widow (Gena Rowlands) fights with a daughter, befriends an abused neighbor's (Marisa Tomei) son and gains a trucker's (Gérard Depardieu) infatuation.

programme.title: Bride and Prejudice (2004)
programme.desc: Mrs Bakshi is looking for suitable husbands for her daughters. When the rich single gents Balraj and Darcy visit, the Bakshis have high hopes, but circumstances get in the way of romance.

programme.title: Memory (2023)
programme.desc: Social worker Sylvia's structured life is blown apart when Saul follows her home from their high school reunion. Their surprise encounter profoundly impacts both of them as they open the door to the past.

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Diary (2001)
programme.desc: An outrageous British woman (Renée Zellweger) decides to take control of her life, but instead falls for two very different men (Colin Firth, Hugh Grant).

programme.title: Parallel (2024)
programme.desc: Following the journey of Vanessa, a grief-stricken woman who mysteriously finds herself navigating between parallel spaces.

programme.title: Wishful Thinking (1996)
programme.desc: A young woman (Drew Barrymore) falls for her best friend (James LeGros) while he looks for the man his girlfriend (Jennifer Beals) is having an affair with.

programme.title: Last Weekend (2014)
programme.desc: Petty squabbling marks the Memorial Day reunion of an affluent matriarch (Patricia Clarkson) and her dysfunctional family (Chris Mulkey, Zachary Booth).

programme.title: Terms of Endearment (1983)
programme.desc: A strong-willed woman (Shirley MacLaine) and her equally independent daughter (Debra Winger) keep the lines of communication open as each deals with the curveballs life throws at them.

programme.title: The Dead Don't Hurt (2023)
programme.desc: When the man she loves, Holger Olsen, goes off to fight in the Civil War, Vivienne Le Coudy must fend for herself in a place controlled by a corrupt mayor and his unscrupulous business partner.

programme.title: Vanilla Sky (2001)
programme.desc: A wealthy womanizer (Tom Cruise) has trouble distinguishing dreams from reality after a car accident leaves him disfigured.

programme.title: Rumours (2024)
programme.desc: Leaders of the world's wealthiest democracies become spectacles of incompetence, contending with increasingly surreal obstacles as night falls in the misty woods, and they realize they are suddenly alone.

programme.title: The General's Daughter (1999)
programme.desc: Two investigators (John Travolta, Madeleine Stowe) find an underworld of sex and cover-ups behind an Army captain's murder.

programme.title: Borrego (2022)
programme.desc: After witnessing a drug mule's plane crash in the Borrego desert, Elly is kidnapped and forced on a dangerous journey to a remote drop-off point.

programme.title: Guns Up (2025)
programme.desc: After a job goes horribly wrong, a man who moonlights as a mob henchman has one night to get his family out of the city while keeping his dark secret hidden.

programme.title: Backcountry (2014)
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: End of Sentence (2019)
programme.desc: After being released from prison, a young man reluctantly embarks on a journey with his estranged father to spread his mother's ashes in her native Ireland.

programme.title: Terms of Endearment (1983)
programme.desc: A strong-willed woman (Shirley MacLaine) and her equally independent daughter (Debra Winger) keep the lines of communication open as each deals with the curveballs life throws at them.

programme.title: The Friend (2024)
programme.desc: Iris finds her comfortable New York City life thrown into disarray after her closest friend and mentor bequeaths her a Great Dane named Apollo. Yet as Iris finds herself unexpectedly bonding with Apollo, she begins to come to terms with her past.

programme.title: Bridget Jones's Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Befuddled mom-to-be Bridget Jones (Renée Zellweger) must figure out which of the two men (Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey) in her life is the father of her child.

programme.title: Jenny's Wedding (2015)
programme.desc: When Jenny (Katherine Heigl) decides to marry a woman (Alexis Bledel), her conventional family must accept who she is or risk losing her forever.

programme.title: Death Becomes Her (1992)
programme.desc: An actress (Meryl Streep) and an author (Goldie Hawn) fight over a plastic surgeon (Bruce Willis) and the secret of eternal beauty.

programme.title: Lara Croft: Tomb Raider (2001)
programme.desc: A beautiful globe-trotter (Angelina Jolie) battles otherworldly creatures for possession of a powerful artifact.

programme.title: An Unfinished Life (2005)
programme.desc: Old wounds get a chance to heal when a Wyoming rancher (Robert Redford) shelters his abused daughter-in-law (Jennifer Lopez) and her child.
